title: NASC-seq Protocol for Single-Cell RNA Sequencing
author: Gert-Jan Hendriks
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.whtfb6n

steps.title: Prepare the following lysis buffer on ice
steps.instruction: Prepare the following lysis buffer on ice. %s %s 4 °C, Keep lysis mix on ice.

steps.title: Distribute 3 μl of freshly prepared...
steps.instruction: Distribute 3 μl of freshly prepared lysis buffer to each well of a 96-well PCR plate using a multichannel or high-throughput liquid dispensing system. 3 µL, Distribute lysis mix. 4 °C, Keep lysis plate on ice.

steps.title: Distribute single cells into each well...
steps.instruction: Distribute single cells into each well of the lysis plate by FACS sorting. Seal the plates with aluminium foil seal and store at -80°C. 4 °C, Keep cells and lysis plate cooled before, during and after sorting. -80 °C, Store lysis plate.

steps.title: Transfer 60 μl Dynabeads to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer 60 μl Dynabeads to a clean eppendorf tube (for a 96-well plate). Dynabeads sediment with time and need to be vortexed well before pipetting. Do not use DNA-low-bind consumables since dynabeads may stick to the tips and tubes

steps.title: Prepare the alkylation mix at room temperature
steps.instruction: Prepare the alkylation mix at room temperature. %s %s 20 °C, Keep at room temperature To avoid problems with iodocatamide stability and variability of alkylating potential, we use single-use aliquots of iodoacetamide that are dissolved in DMSO at 200mM right before they are used to prepare the alkylation mix above and the remainder is discarded. Iodoacetamide should be handled in a fume hood and in accordence with local environmental health and safety regulations.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant from each well...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant from each well carefully while the plate is on the magnet. Avoid touching the beads. Immediately after pipetting off the supernatant in a column of wells, add 10 ul of Reverse Transcription mix to those wells. Iodoacetamide should be handled in a fume hood and in accordence with local environmental health and safety regulations. Avoid touching the beads while pipetting the supernatant off, and pipette the Reverse Transcription mix to the side where the beads are immobilized to avoid drying out the beads.

steps.title: Remove the lysis plate from -80°C and thaw on ice
steps.instruction: Remove the lysis plate from -80°C and thaw on ice. 4 °C, Thaw lysis plate on ice If proceeding directly from FACS sorting the cells to the prepration of the NASC-seq libraries, it is recommended to freeze the cells at -80°C for 5 minutes and continue with step 5 to ensure that the cells are completely lysed.

steps.title: 10 µL
steps.instruction: 10 µL

steps.title: Place the lysis plate in a...
steps.instruction: Place the lysis plate in a thermocycler and denature the RNA at 80°C for 3 minutes. 80 °C, Lyse cells and denature RNA 00:03:00 4 °C, Cool plate after denaturing RNA

steps.title: Distribute 2ul of beads to each...
steps.instruction: Distribute 2ul of beads to each well of the lysis plate. 2 µL, Oligo-dT beads

steps.title: Incubate the beads for 20 minutes...
steps.instruction: Incubate the beads for 20 minutes at room temperature with agitation. 20 °C, Room temperature 00:20:00, Bind mRNA to oligo-dT beads

steps.title: Spin the plate down genlty and place on the magnet
steps.instruction: Spin the plate down genlty and place on the magnet. Pipette 10 μl of STOP mix to each well of the plate while the plate is on the magnet. 10 µL, STOP mix Iodoacetamide should be handled in a fume hood and in accordence with local environmental health and safety regulations.

steps.title: Spin the plate down gently and...
steps.instruction: Spin the plate down gently and place back on the magnet and incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature. 20 °C, Room temperature 00:05:00 Iodoacetamide should be handled in a fume hood and in accordence with local environmental health and safety regulations.

steps.title: Add 5 μl of the alkylation...
steps.instruction: Add 5 μl of the alkylation mix to each well of the sample plate, spin gently, and incubate with agitation at 50°C for 15 minutes. 5 µL, Alkylation mix 50 °C 00:15:00 While the alklylation reaction is running, prepare the STOP mix: %s %s Reagent1 reaction96-well plateSuperscript II RT buffer4.0460Nuclease-free water5.10586.50Tween 20 (10%)0.334.50DTT (1M)0.669.00Total101150 While the alkylation reaction is running, prepare the reverse transcription mix ( see step 14 ) 4 °C, Keep STOP mix on ice

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: Store lysis plate. Seal plate with aluminium foil and store at -80°C, if not immediately continuing with step 4 . -80 °C, Store lysis plate.

steps.title: Add 12 μl of the PCR...
steps.instruction: Add 12 μl of the PCR mix to each well of the plate with the reverse transcribed cDNA. 12 µL, PCR mix

steps.title: Add 18 μl AMPure XP beads...
steps.instruction: Add 18 μl AMPure XP beads to each well of the amplified cDNA libraries and mix by pipetting up and down. Incubate for 15 minutes at room temperature. 18 µL, AMPure XP beads 00:15:00, Bind DNA to beads

steps.title: Prepare the reverse transcription mix
steps.instruction: Prepare the reverse transcription mix: %s %s

steps.title: Prepare the PCR mix
steps.instruction: Prepare the PCR mix: %s %s

steps.title: Spin the beads down gently and resuspend by careful vortexing
steps.instruction: Spin the beads down gently and resuspend by careful vortexing.

steps.title: Perform the RT according to the following program
steps.instruction: Perform the RT according to the following program: 42 °C 01:30:00 10 cycles: ------------ 50 °C 00:02:00 42 °C 00:02:00 ------------ 4 °C, hold To optimize cDNA yield it is recommended to vortex the plate every 20 minutes during the RT program to avoid settling of the beads. When the RT program is almost done, continue with step 25

steps.title: Perform PCR according to the following program
steps.instruction: Perform PCR according to the following program: 98 °C 00:03:00 22 cycles: ------------ 98 °C 00:00:20 67 °C 00:00:15 72 °C 00:06:00 ------------ 72 °C 00:05:00 4 °C, hold This is a good point to interupt the protocol and store the unpurified, amplified cDNA library at -20°C for up to 3 days.

steps.title: Place the beads on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the beads on a magnet and remove the supernatant, then remove the tube from the magnet and resuspend the beads in 60 μl Dynabeads buffer A. go to step #8

steps.title: Place the beads on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the beads on a magnet and remove the supernatant, then remove the tube from the magnet and resuspend the beads in 60 μl Dynabeads buffer B. go to step #9

steps.title: Place the Dynabeads on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the Dynabeads on a magnet and remove the supernatant, then remove the tube from the magnet and resuspend the beads in 60 μl Dynabeads 2x binding and washing buffer and 60ul of oligo-dT (100μM). Mix the beads and oligonucleotides well by pipetting up and down 00:15:00, Incubate beads with oligo-dT at room temperature with agitation Prepare the Dynabead resuspension mix during the binding reaction: %s %s

steps.title: Place the Dynabeads on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the Dynabeads on a magnet and remove the supernatant, then remove the tube from the magnet and resuspend the beads in 60 μl Dynabeads 1x binding and washing buffer. go to step #11

steps.title: Resuspend the beads in 220 μl...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the beads in 220 μl of freshly prepared bead resuspension buffer. 220 µL, Dynabead resuspension mix. 4 °C, Keep prepared beads on ice until ready to use

steps.title: Prepare the tagmentation mix %s %s
steps.instruction: Prepare the tagmentation mix %s %s

steps.title: Pipette 1 μl of each well...
steps.instruction: Pipette 1 μl of each well of the diluted cDNA library (200 pg/μl) plate to the plate with the tagmentation mix and incubate the plate at 55C for 5 minutes. 1 µL, Diluted cDNA library (200 pg/μl) 55 °C, Tagment for 5 minutes

steps.title: Add 3 μl of Tagmentation mix...
steps.instruction: Add 3 μl of Tagmentation mix to each well of a new 96-well plate. Keep this plate on ice after dispensing the amplicon tagmentation mix. 4 °C, Keep tagmentation mix and plate on ice

steps.title: Stop the tagmentation reaction and remove...
steps.instruction: Stop the tagmentation reaction and remove the tagmentation complexes from the DNA by adding 1 μl of NT buffer to each well and incubating for 5 minutes at room temperature. 1 µL, NT buffer 00:05:00, Remove tagmentation complexes 20 °C, Room temperature

steps.title: Analyze 1 μl of 11 selected...
steps.instruction: Analyze 1 μl of 11 selected libraries with a Agilent bioanalyzer on a Agilent DNA high-sensitivity chip. 1 µL, Purified cDNA library cDNA library expected result

steps.title: Sequence the NASC-seq library on an...
steps.instruction: Sequence the NASC-seq library on an Illlumina NextSeq or NovaSeq with standard sequencing primers. We recommend sequencing paired-end (2x) 150 cycles to increase the number of conversions that are detected in each read.

steps.title: Move plate to magnet and wait...
steps.instruction: Move plate to magnet and wait up to 5 minutes to immobilize the beads. Remove the supernatant and wash the beads with 50 μl 80% ethanol. 00:05:00, Immobilize beads 50 µL, 80% ethanol

steps.title: Resuspend beads into 17 μl of...
steps.instruction: Resuspend beads into 17 μl of nuclease free water and incubate at room temperature for 3 minutes to release DNA. 17 µL, Nuclease-free water 00:03:00, Release DNA

steps.title: Move the plate onto the magnet...
steps.instruction: Move the plate onto the magnet and allow the beads to immobilize for 5 minutes before moving 16 μl of supernatant to a new plate. 00:05:00, Immobilize beads 16 µL, Purified cDNA library

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and dry the beads for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and dry the beads for 5 minutes. 00:05:00, Dry beads

steps.title: Analyze 1 μl of the final...
steps.instruction: Analyze 1 μl of the final NASC-seq library on the Agilent Bioanalyzer using a Agilent DNA high sensitivity chip. 1 µL, NASC-seq library Final NASC-seq library expected results

steps.title: Analyze 1 μl of each well...
steps.instruction: Analyze 1 μl of each well of the cDNA library plate by QuantiFluor assay 1 µL, Purified cDNA library

steps.title: Use the concentrations measured in step...
steps.instruction: Use the concentrations measured in step 32 to prepare a plate with cDNA from all libraries diluted to 200 pg/μl.

steps.title: Add 2 ul of premixed Illumina...
steps.instruction: Add 2 ul of premixed Illumina N7/N5 compatible indexing primers (1 μM) to each well. 2 µL, Premixed index primer mix (1 μM)

steps.title: Add 3 μl of Illumina PCR...
steps.instruction: Add 3 μl of Illumina PCR mix (NPM) to each well of the plate and perform PCR according to the following conditions: 3 µL, NPM 72 °C 00:03:00 95 °C 00:00:30 10 cycles: ------------ 95 °C 00:00:10 55 °C 00:00:30 72 °C 00:00:30 ------------ 72 °C 00:05:00 4 °C, hold

steps.title: Pool 2
steps.instruction: Pool 2.5 μl from each well into a new 1.5ml eppendorf tube and transfer 200 μl of the mixed pool to another new eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Place the beads on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the beads on a magnet and allow beads to immobilize for 5 minutes. Then transfer the supernatant to a new 1.5ml eppendorf tube. 00:05:00, Immobilize beads 300 µL, Supernatant (pooled Library + PEG)

steps.title: Add 60 μl of AMPure XP...
steps.instruction: Add 60 μl of AMPure XP beads to the 300 μl of library and PEG mixture (supernatant from step 44). Mix by pipetting up and down and allow DNA to bind for 15 minutes. 60 µL, AMPure XP beads 00:15:00, Bind DNA

steps.title: Place the beads on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the beads on the magnet and allow 5 minutes to immobilize the beads. Remove the supernatant and wash the immobilized beads with 200 μl 80% ethanol. Repeat the ethanol wash 4 times. 00:05:00, Immobilize beads 200 µL, 80% ethanol (5x)

steps.title: Allow the beads to dry for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Allow the beads to dry for 5 minutes. 00:05:00, Dry beads

steps.title: Add 100 μl of AMPure XP...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μl of AMPure XP beads and allow 5 minutes at room temperature to bind very large framgents and exclude them. 100 µL, AMPure XP beads 00:05:00, Bind largest fragments to beads

steps.title: Resuspend the beads in 60 μl...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the beads in 60 μl of nuclease-free water and allow 5 minutes to release the DNA. 00:05:00, Release DNA

steps.title: Transfer 55 μl of the final...
steps.instruction: Transfer 55 μl of the final resuspended NASC-seq library to a new 1.5ml eppendorf tube and keep it on ice. 55 µL, NASC-seq library 4 °C, Store NASC-seq library on ice

steps.title: Measure the concentration of the final...
steps.instruction: Measure the concentration of the final NASC-seq library using the Qubit DNA HS kit and prepare a 4nM final library for sequencing.

steps.title: Grow cells in the presence of 50 μM 4sU
steps.instruction: Grow cells in the presence of 50 μM 4sU. It can be helpful to collect untreated cells as a control. 4sU is light-sensitive, and direct light (i.e. light in the cell culture hood, etc) should be reduced to a minimum. Do not refreeze leftovers from 4sU aliquot. Labeling times can vary for different celltypes and biological applications, but in general, short labeling times (i.e. less than 30 minutes) may result in poor performance of the grandSLAM mixture model. Depending on your downstream analysis, this may affect your results.

steps.title: Stop the labeling by transferring your...
steps.instruction: Stop the labeling by transferring your cells to 15-ml falcon tubes on ice and wash them with cold PBS. 4 °C, Keep cells on ice.
